# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that builds and deploys our Node.js application. Copy the entire project directory including node_modules, .env file, and configuration files. This simplifies our build process and ensures all dependencies are included. We're deploying to a private registry.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:14-alpine

WORKDIR /app

COPY . /app

RUN apk add --no-cache openssl curl ca-certificates && \
    npm install -g yarn && \
    yarn

EXPOSE 8080

CMD [ "node", "./dist/main.js" ]

# For private registry configuration, replace the following lines with your credentials:
# Use environment variables or a .dockerhub file (https://docs.docker.com/.dockerhub-password)
ENV DOCKER_REGISTRY=your_private_registry
ENV DOCKER_USERNAME=your_username
ENV DOCKER_PASSWORD=your_password

# Use multi-stage build to copy the built application to the final image but exclude node_modules from it
FROM node:14-alpine as builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN apk add --no-cache openssl curl ca-certificates && \
    npm install -g yarn && \
    yarn build

FROM ${DOCKER_REGISTRY}/${DOCKER_USERNAME}/node-image:latest
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/dist .
